scope: submitted-build hash slots for certification freeze and lab package assembly
freeze_policy: every submitted artifact must have a SHA-256 digest before certification-facing review
the submitted binary, signed profiles, CAPKs, vectors, RTM, reports, traces, and approval package must name the same product scope
a changed artifact hash requires a new freeze review and supersedes the prior package by recorded reason
the freeze manifest is a binding template only and cannot close external certification blockers by itself

required_artifacts.title: Submitted kernel binary
required_artifacts.evidence_source: release build pipeline artifact digest accepted for the lab submission

required_artifacts.title: Signed runtime configuration bundle
required_artifacts.evidence_source: signed configuration package digest tied to the submitted binary

required_artifacts.title: Scheme/acquirer-approved CAPK bundle
required_artifacts.evidence_source: accepted CAPK package digest with signed provenance

required_artifacts.title: Scheme/acquirer-approved profile bundle
required_artifacts.evidence_source: accepted scheme profile package digest with profile authority evidence

required_artifacts.title: Lab-supplied ODA and APDU test-vector bundle
Full masked APDU and outcome trace pack
required_artifacts.evidence_source: recognized-lab or accepted test-tool masked trace-pack digest

required_artifacts.title: Final RTM and lab/tool crosswalk
required_artifacts.evidence_source: final RTM digest after lab test-case ID reconciliation

required_artifacts.title: Accepted 100% coverage report package
required_artifacts.evidence_source: accepted coverage report and metadata package digest

required_artifacts.title: Accepted static-analysis and fuzzing report package
required_artifacts.evidence_source: accepted static-analysis and fuzzing report package digest

required_artifacts.title: Signed approval and conformance package
required_artifacts.evidence_source: recognized authority signed approval package digest
